---
title: 'Gateway Personal Ministry W/ Luke and Richard'
date: '2015-01-31T16:49:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7086653154861047169'
blogger_filename: '/2015/01/gateway-personal-ministry-w-luke-and.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Word From God'
  - 'Journal'
  - 'Counsel'
  - 'kairos'
  - 'Life Events'
---

So on 01/28/15, I went to Gateway Personal Ministry. I didn't know what to expect. It was a little scary. After getting lost twice, and ending up inside the wrong Gateway office, I finally reached my destination. I was ushered into the room with two men. One about my age, maybe younger, and one about Rocky's age.<br />
<br />
After some introductions, and commentary on my Superman Shirt, we got to talking. They had reviewed my intro sheet I filled out ahead of time, and commented that I was an open book. I stated that I was, in fact, you can read what I'm thinking on my blog, I keep my life open.<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
Belief - Thought - Action</h4>
<br />
So, as we got to talking, the first thing that comes to memory, is the fact that Gateway Freedom Ministries has several core beliefs. One of them is that we cannot deal with matters of life through behavior, but through belief! That goes make to the Levels of Change Foundation Class. Actions come from the variety of thoughts we have. Thoughts are like branches that grow from the root of a belief. You cannot deal with change at the level of actions or thoughts, as these just manifest in different ways. When one is cut down, the next pops up. You cut off Alcohol, and you are addicted to Dr. Pepper without realizing it. You cannot deal with the substance, addiction, or behavior. That is irrelevant. You cannot deal with the thoughts "I really want a ...." because that too, is a symptom. You must, through the Holy Spirit, reveal the belief(s) that are giving life to those thoughts, which are influencing those behaviors.<br />
<br />
Beliefs gives birth to thoughts, thoughts give birth to actions, and the resulting events that arise from those actions reinforce the belief. This can be both a negative and/or positive cycle, depending on what belief is in question.<br />
<br />
If I believe that gravity causes me to fall, and I have thoughts that if I jump off of tables, ladders, cars, roofs, I will fall... then I do some or all of these things and it happens. This reinforces my belief.<br />
<br />
Attempting to change thoughts or actions without changing the belief root is ineffective at best.<br />
<h4>
<br /></h4>
<h4>
Hearing</h4>
<br />
Don't ask "Is this God, or is this me?" This is the wrong question for many reasons.<br />
<br />
1. God Speaks To and Through Your Uniqueness.<br />
<br />
God doesn't just speak in sentences, although he can and does do that. He also speaks in words, images, visions, dreams, impressions, ideas, concepts, and more. God has also given you a set of gifts, talents, and perspectives that, when put together, are you uniquely yours. Then God puts ideas into your heart that align with the way He's built you.<br />
<br />
So when I have an idea for a story, or to go to certain place to minister to someone, or whatever, it very well may sound like me. This because it is coming from God, through my gifting, and resonating (bouncing around off of all of my giftings and talents) in me.<br />
<br />
2. There were two trees. Knowledge and Life.<br />
<br />
When man operates without God, it looks like Knowledge. Religion attempts to overcome the knowledge of Evil with the knowledge of Good. But the Knowledge of Good will never re-connect you to LIFE, which is the real need of man. <br />
<br />
So do not attempt to find the will or voice of God through Knowledge. "Is this God or is this me?" attempts to rationalize the voice of God. This is almost like saying "Does two plus two equal purple?" The question makes no sense. This is because (as pointed in #1) we are built in a specific way, and God speaks to us through our uniqueness. Also, we are built in the image of God. As with a branch on a tree, as we get closer to Him, it gets harder and harder to identify where one ends and the other begins. You can clearly see that the branch is not the tree, and that the branch can be cut from the tree. But can you honestly say that you can find the exact point at which a branch ceases to be the branch and the tree begins? The joining point is so integrated, that is become difficult to distinguish the point of separation. <br />
<br />
<br />
I feel "stuck" in my Spiritual life.<br />
<br />
How you do you define stuck?<br />
<br />
I get real fired up for God, and spiritual things. I'm in my bible and prayer time every morning. Then after a number of weeks or months that wanes, and I wake up one day to find that I haven't read my bible in months. <br />
<br />
Have you considered that it may be God, trying to get you to put your bible down and notebook down?<br />
<br />
??<br />
<br />
You stated that you are an intellectual. You study. You study the words, the meanings, the history... etc. God gave you those gifts and they are good. But consider this. If you have read someone's book, and their Facebook profile. You know all their stats. Do you KNOW them, do you have a living vibrant relationship with them (<a href="http://youtu.be/qM-gZintWDc" target="_blank">good will hunting</a>)(couldn't find the scene "yeah I got plenty of mentors, Niche, Plato, etc")? No, you just know about them. You don't know them. <br />
<br />
If the book is not leading you to the author, put the book down, and go after the author. You are so intellectual, that your disinterest in reading the bible during your "devotional time" may be the Holy Spirit himself leading you to put the bible down.<br />
<br />
This is not to say that bible study is bad, or that you should stop it forever. This is to say that God may want you to let Him out of the box, so that He can speak with you in the 1,000's of ways that He wants to, instead of the three or four (Bible, Journal, Prayer, Study) that you put Him into.<br />
<br />
If you are experiencing this cycle of Spiritual Connectedness, followed by a disinterest in your usual routine, then flowing into apathy, and this cycle has repeated itself since you were a pre-teen. STOP the cycle!<br />
<br />
God is not interested in what you do, He is interested in who you are! He is not looking for better behaviors, traditions, or rituals, he is looking for a vibrant relationship.<br />
<br />
Hearing is an experience. You can't get hearing from reading. You can hear while you read, He can speak to you through the words you read. But they are two separate things. God can just as easily speak to your heart during a class taught by an atheist, as He can through a sermon. But this will only happen if you are keeping your lines of connection open and unplugged. <br />
<br />
<h4>
Mirrors</h4>
<br />
You cannot know what you look like without a mirror. In the natural, you cannot know what you look like without a mirror. How will you know about the smudge on your forehead without seeing it in a mirror? If someone tells you that it's there, how will you know they are telling the truth? (I've seen people say it's there when it's not just to mess with people).<br />
<br />
God is your mirror. You are made in his image. When you get close to Him, he reveals who and what you are. But unlike a natural mirror, when you get close to God, you also become more of who and what you are.<br />
<br />
Other humans can help with this process, they can point to the smudge they see, that you do not. But some humans have a warped image, and therefore give you a warped reflection. Therefore, you have to rely primarily on God as the revealing image, and only those humans He leads you to. <br />
<br />
<br />
Suggested I take a look at <a href="http://lifestylechristianity.com/connect/#" target="_blank">Todd White</a> and <a href="http://brilliantperspectives.com/" target="_blank">Graham Cooke</a>. <br />
<br />
Thought: When the heart is connected to LOVE, fear looses it's rational grip.<br />
<br />
My Achiever and Intellection strengths have the tendency to make my relationship with God a process (religion) as opposed to the vibrant flowing relationship that He desires.<br />
<br />
Past Words from God:<br />
<br />
In the past years, I've had God ask of me some interesting things. <br />
<ul>
<li>Sing to me like you did when you were younger.</li>
<li>Tell me a story.</li>
<li>Spend some time in the mornings being still. Just be still, no reading, praying, and be still.Be still and <i>know </i>that I am God. This is one way to go from knowing about God to knowing God.</li>
</ul>
It occurs to me know, that they are all centered on getting out of routine and into relationship.&nbsp; <br />
<h4>
Assignment:&nbsp;</h4>
<br />
Be still, be led, be open to the weird and extra-ordinary ways God may want to communicate with you. Follow the desires that come to your heart. If you feel he just says "go for a walk", "listen to Lion King sound tracks", etc, don't question it, just flow with it.<br />
<br />
Be Still - Be Led - Be Open. <br />
<br />
.....
